seoTitle: Holsworthy vs Home Rule — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Holsworthy vs Home Rule: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Holsworthy and Home Rule.
heading: Holsworthy vs Home Rule — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Holsworthy, New South Wales and Home Rule, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Home Rule has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Holsworthy (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Holsworthy or Home Rule safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Holsworthy has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Home Rule scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Holsworthy scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Home Rule scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Holsworthy or Home Rule?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Home Rule has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
